header.title: Select
header.subtitle: Native dropdown field built on the browser's select element. Supports labels, error states, placeholder, and all standard HTML select attributes.

anatomy.title: Anatomy
anatomy.description: A select is composed of an optional label, the native select field with a chevron icon, and optional error text.

parts.label: Identifies the field. Rendered as a label element with matching htmlFor. Optional but recommended for accessibility.
parts.field: The native select element. Accepts all standard HTML select attributes.
parts.icon: ChevronDown icon positioned in the trailing slot. Decorative — pointer events are disabled.
parts.helper: Error message rendered below the field when the error prop is set.

states.title: States
states.description: The select inherits the same border and focus styles as Input. Error state overrides the border color and focus ring.

withLabel.title: With label
withLabel.description: Pass a label prop to render an accessible label above the field. The id is auto-generated from the label text.

placeholder.title: Placeholder
placeholder.description: Pass a placeholder prop to show a non-selectable first option as a prompt.

roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.description: The current Select is native-first: it uses the browser's built-in dropdown, which guarantees accessibility, mobile support, and zero extra JavaScript. Custom listbox features (search, multi-select, grouped options, async loading) are planned for a future release.

props.title: Props
props.description: Extends all native select attributes. Component-specific props listed below.

props.label: Label displayed above the field. Generates an id automatically.
props.error: Error message shown in red below the field. Also applies error styling to the border.
props.placeholder: Non-selectable first option used as a prompt when no value is selected.
props.extendsNote: Extends all native select attributes (name, multiple, size, …).
